meta.description: Example profile: rural properties under $200K in upstate New York. The scorer reads this before evaluating each listing.

price_scoring._comment: Tiered scoring. Cheaper = better, but don't lose good ones at the top of range.
price_scoring.scoring_note: Under 120K gets a score boost. 120K-200K scored normally. Over 200K is a hard reject.

surface_scoring._comment: Bigger is better at this price range.
surface_scoring.scoring_note: Under 800 sq ft is a hard reject. 800-1,199 sq ft scored normally. 1,200-1,799 sq ft gets a mild boost. 1,800+ sq ft gets a stronger boost. If surface is not listed, flag it but don't reject.

target_regions.scoring_note: Primary regions (Hudson Valley, Catskills, Berkshires) get a score boost. Secondary regions scored normally. NYC metro, Long Island, New Jersey = reject (wrong market).

accepted: fixer-upper (if price is very low, under $60K)
condition.scoring_note: Renovation-ready is PREFERRED. A larger fixer-upper scores higher than a smaller turnkey at the same price. Habitable but dated is also good. The buyer is comfortable with renovation and actively looking for underpriced space.
condition.preference: Bigger + needs work > Smaller + move-in ready.

proximity_scoring._comment: Distance to towns and amenities matters for rural properties.
proximity_scoring.scoring_note: Under 10 miles from a town with grocery/hospital gets a boost. 10-20 miles scored normally. Over 20 miles gets a mild penalty. No road access is a hard reject.

flags_not_rejects._comment: Surface these with a note, do not auto-reject.
items: well water instead of municipal (common in rural areas, not a dealbreaker)
septic system instead of sewer (same as above)
no central heating (wood stove or space heaters, needs upgrade)
unpaved/gravel driveway (normal for rural, not an issue)
surface area not listed (could be fine, needs manual check)
property tax over $5K/year (note for budget planning)

features_not_scored._comment: Buyer is early in research. These are noted but don't affect score yet.

scoring_thresholds.preference: Prefer false negatives over false positives. Buyer's time is limited.

output_format.notable_features: list of observed features from the features_not_scored list
